commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And the king spoke unto Ashpenaz the master of his eunuchs, that he should bring certain of the children of Israel, and of the king's descendants, and of the princes; Eunuchs, or chief officers. The Jews assert that Daniel was made an eunuch, Isaias xxxix. But he might be so styled on account of his dignity. (Calmet) Princes. Literally, "tyrants. "(Haydock) This name was afterwards only rendered odious by the misconduct of several kings. (Calmet) Hebrew parthemim, (Haydock) seems to be of Greek derivation, alluding to protimoi, or protoi, "the first or most honoured. "(Drusius) We find here other Greek words.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
